column2:
menganalisis
To examine something in detail in order to understand it.
Synonyms: examine, study, investigate.
Scientists analyze the data to find patterns in climate change.

analisis
A detailed examination of something to understand it better.
Synonyms: examination, study, evaluation.
The analysis of the survey results took several weeks to complete.

pendekatan
A way of dealing with a situation or problem.
Synonyms: method, strategy, way.
Teachers should adopt a flexible approach to suit different learners.

bidang; wilayah
A particular subject or part of a place.
Synonyms: field, region, zone.
Renewable energy is an important area of scientific research today.

menilai
To judge the quality or value of something carefully.
Synonyms: evaluate, judge, measure.
Teachers assess students' progress through regular tests and assignments.

penilaian
The act of judging or evaluating something.
Synonyms: evaluation, appraisal, review.
Continuous assessment is more useful than a single final exam.

menganggap; mengasumsikan
To accept something is true without proof.
Synonyms: suppose, presume, believe.
Many people assume that technology always improves our quality of life.

otoritas; pihak berwenang
The power or right to make decisions and give orders.
Synonyms: power, control, government.
Local authorities are responsible for managing public health services.

tersedia
Able to be obtained, used, or accessed.
Synonyms: accessible, obtainable, ready.
Free online courses are now available to students around the world.

manfaat; keuntungan
An advantage or positive effect from something.
Synonyms: advantage, gain, profit.
Regular exercise has many benefits for both body and mind.

konsep
An abstract idea or general notion.
Synonyms: idea, notion, theory.
The concept of sustainability is central to modern environmental policy.

terdiri dari
To be made up of certain parts or things.
Synonyms: comprise, contain, include.
A balanced diet should consist of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains.

merupakan; membentuk
To be or form something.
Synonyms: form, comprise, make up.
Women constitute nearly half of the global workforce today.

konteks
The situation or background that helps explain something.
Synonyms: background, setting, situation.
It is important to consider the historical context of the war.

kontrak
A written legal agreement between people or organizations.
Synonyms: agreement, deal, pact.
The company signed a contract to build a new high school.

menciptakan
To make something new or bring it into existence.
Synonyms: produce, generate, make.
Artists create works that reflect the society they live in.

data
Facts or information collected for analysis.
Synonyms: information, statistics, facts.
Researchers collected data from over 5,000 students for the study.

mendefinisikan
To explain the exact meaning of something.
Synonyms: describe, specify, explain.
It is hard to define success because it means different things to people.

memperoleh; berasal dari
To get or obtain something from a source.
Synonyms: obtain, get, gain.
Many English words derive from Latin and Greek roots.

mendistribusikan
To give or share things among people or places.
Synonyms: hand out, share, deliver.
Volunteers distribute food to families affected by the flood.

ekonomi
The system of money, trade, and industry in a country.
Synonyms: financial system, market, trade.
The country's economy depends heavily on tourism and agriculture.

lingkungan
The natural world or surroundings in which we live.
Synonyms: surroundings, ecosystem, setting.
Plastic pollution is harming the environment in every ocean.

mendirikan; menetapkan
To start or create something that will last.
Synonyms: set up, found, create.
The university was established more than a hundred years ago.

memperkirakan
To make an approximate calculation of something.
Synonyms: calculate, guess, predict.
Experts estimate that sea levels will rise significantly by 2050.

bukti
Facts or information that prove something is true.
Synonyms: proof, support, indication.
There is strong evidence that smoking causes serious health problems.

mengekspor
To sell goods to another country.
Synonyms: ship, send, sell abroad.
Indonesia exports large amounts of palm oil to Europe each year.

faktor
A condition or thing that influences a result.
Synonyms: element, cause, reason.
Diet and exercise are key factors in maintaining good health.

keuangan
The management of money, especially by governments or companies.
Synonyms: money, funds, economics.
Personal finance should be taught in every secondary school.

fungsi
The purpose or role of something.
Synonyms: purpose, role, use.
The main function of schools is to prepare children for adult life.

mengidentifikasi
To recognize and name someone or something.
Synonyms: recognize, spot, name.
Doctors can now identify many diseases at an early stage.

penghasilan; pendapatan
Money received from work or investments.
Synonyms: earnings, salary, revenue.
Families with low income struggle to pay for higher education.

menunjukkan
To show or point out something clearly.
Synonyms: show, suggest, signal.
The graph indicates a steady rise in global temperatures.

menafsirkan
To explain the meaning of something.
Synonyms: explain, understand, translate.
Different readers may interpret the same poem in different ways.

melibatkan
To include something as a necessary part.
Synonyms: include, contain, require.
Learning a language involves daily practice and patience.

isu; masalah
An important problem or topic for discussion.
Synonyms: problem, matter, topic.
Climate change is one of the biggest issues of our time.

legal; sah menurut hukum
Allowed or required by the law.
Synonyms: lawful, permitted, legitimate.
It is not legal to drive a car without a valid license.

utama; besar
Very important, large, or serious.
Synonyms: main, primary, significant.
Air pollution is a major cause of respiratory diseases in cities.

metode; cara
A particular way of doing something.
Synonyms: way, approach, technique.
This teaching method has helped many weak students improve quickly.

terjadi
To happen, often without being planned.
Synonyms: happen, take place, arise.
Earthquakes often occur along the boundaries of tectonic plates.

periode; masa
A length of time.
Synonyms: time, era, phase.
The Renaissance was a period of great cultural change in Europe.

kebijakan
A set of plans or rules used by a government or organization.
Synonyms: rules, plan, strategy.
The government's new policy aims to reduce youth unemployment.

prinsip
A basic rule or belief that guides behavior.
Synonyms: rule, value, standard.
Honesty is an important principle in both work and friendship.

melanjutkan; melangsungkan
To continue an action or move forward.
Synonyms: continue, advance, move on.
After a short break, the students proceed with their writing exam.

proses
A series of actions taken to achieve a result.
Synonyms: procedure, operation, method.
Learning a foreign language is a slow but rewarding process.

memerlukan; mengharuskan
To need something or make something necessary.
Synonyms: need, demand, request.
Most universities require students to pass an English language test.

riset; penelitian
A careful study to discover new facts or information.
Synonyms: study, investigation, inquiry.
Recent research suggests that screen time affects children's sleep.

merespons; menanggapi
To say or do something as a reaction.
Synonyms: reply, answer, react.
Governments must respond quickly to natural disasters.

peran
The function or part someone or something plays.
Synonyms: part, function, position.
Parents play an important role in their children's education.

sektor
A part of a country's economy or society.
Synonyms: area, field, branch.
The technology sector has created millions of new jobs worldwide.

signifikan; penting
Important or large enough to be noticed.
Synonyms: important, notable, considerable.
There has been a significant improvement in air quality this year.

serupa; mirip
Almost the same as something else.
Synonyms: alike, comparable, related.
The two students gave similar answers to the difficult question.

sumber
The place where something comes from.
Synonyms: origin, root, supplier.
The sun is the main source of energy for life on Earth.

spesifik; khusus
Clear, exact, or particular.
Synonyms: particular, exact, precise.
Please give a specific example to support your argument in the essay.

struktur
The way parts of something are arranged or organized.
Synonyms: arrangement, framework, organization.
A clear essay structure helps the reader follow your ideas easily.

teori
An idea or set of ideas used to explain something.
Synonyms: hypothesis, idea, principle.
Einstein's theory of relativity changed how we understand the universe.

bervariasi; berbeda-beda
To be different or to change in different situations.
Synonyms: differ, change, range.
Test scores vary widely depending on how much students prepare.

mencapai; meraih
To succeed in doing or getting something.
Synonyms: accomplish, attain, reach.
Hard work and patience help students achieve their academic goals.

memperoleh
To get or develop something over time.
Synonyms: obtain, gain, get.
Children acquire their first language naturally from their parents.

memengaruhi
To cause a change in something or someone.
Synonyms: influence, impact, change.
Lack of sleep can seriously affect a student's performance at school.

sesuai; tepat
Suitable or right for a particular situation.
Synonyms: suitable, fitting, proper.
It is important to use appropriate language in formal essays.

aspek
One part or feature of a situation or topic.
Synonyms: feature, element, side.
Vocabulary is just one aspect of learning a foreign language well.

membantu
To help someone do something.
Synonyms: help, support, aid.
Volunteers assist elderly people with their daily shopping.

kategori
A group of things that share similar features.
Synonyms: group, class, type.
Books in the library are arranged by category and author.

komunitas; masyarakat
A group of people living in the same area or sharing something.
Synonyms: society, public, neighborhood.
Online communities allow students to study together from different countries.

kompleks; rumit
Made of many connected parts; not simple.
Synonyms: complicated, intricate, difficult.
Climate change is a complex problem with no easy solutions.

menyimpulkan
To decide something based on information.
Synonyms: deduce, infer, finish.
The researchers conclude that exercise improves mental health.

kesimpulan
A final decision or judgment after thinking about something.
Synonyms: deduction, ending, result.
Always restate your main idea in the conclusion of an essay.

melakukan; melaksanakan
To organize and carry out an activity.
Synonyms: carry out, perform, run.
Scientists conduct experiments to test their hypotheses.

konsekuensi; akibat
A result or effect of an action or condition.
Synonyms: result, effect, outcome.
Skipping breakfast often has negative consequences for concentration.

membangun; menyusun
To build or form something by joining parts.
Synonyms: build, form, create.
Engineers construct safer bridges using modern technology.

mengkonsumsi
To use, eat, or drink something.
Synonyms: use, eat, drink.
Modern households consume far more electricity than they did fifty years ago.

merancang; mendesain
To plan and create something for a purpose.
Synonyms: plan, create, develop.
Architects design buildings that are both useful and beautiful.

elemen; unsur
One of the basic parts of something.
Synonyms: part, component, factor.
Trust is a key element of every healthy friendship.

mengevaluasi
To judge the value or quality of something.
Synonyms: assess, judge, review.
Teachers evaluate students' essays based on clear criteria.

fitur; ciri
An important part or quality of something.
Synonyms: characteristic, aspect, trait.
One feature of modern smartphones is voice recognition.

akhir; final
Coming at the end; last.
Synonyms: last, ultimate, concluding.
The final exam will determine each student's overall grade.

fokus; memusatkan
To give attention to one thing.
Synonyms: concentrate, center, aim.
Try to focus on the main idea before reading the details.

dampak
A strong effect or influence on something.
Synonyms: effect, influence, consequence.
Social media has a huge impact on young people's behavior.

institut; lembaga
An organization for education or research.
Synonyms: organization, academy, foundation.
The institute offers free English classes to local students.

menginvestasikan
To put money or effort into something to gain benefit.
Synonyms: spend, fund, dedicate.
Parents invest a lot of time and money in their children's education.

memelihara; mempertahankan
To keep something in good condition or continue something.
Synonyms: keep, preserve, sustain.
Regular exercise helps you maintain a healthy body and mind.

memperoleh
To get something, especially with effort.
Synonyms: get, acquire, gain.
You must obtain a visa before traveling to that country.

berpartisipasi
To take part in an activity with others.
Synonyms: join, engage, take part.
All students should participate in classroom discussions actively.

menganggap; merasakan
To notice or understand something.
Synonyms: see, notice, understand.
Many people perceive technology as both helpful and harmful.

positif
Good, hopeful, or showing agreement.
Synonyms: good, optimistic, helpful.
Reading every day has a positive effect on your vocabulary.

potensi
The possibility to develop or succeed in the future.
Synonyms: capability, possibility, promise.
Every child has the potential to become a great learner.

sebelumnya
Existing or happening before something else.
Synonyms: former, earlier, prior.
Her previous job was teaching English at a small school.

utama; primer
Most important or first in order.
Synonyms: main, chief, principal.
The primary cause of obesity is poor diet and lack of exercise.

jangkauan; rentang
A set of similar things or the limits of something.
Synonyms: variety, scope, span.
The library offers a wide range of books on different subjects.

wilayah; daerah
A particular area of a country or the world.
Synonyms: area, zone, district.
This region produces some of the best coffee in the world.

relevan; berkaitan
Closely connected to the topic being discussed.
Synonyms: related, connected, applicable.
Make sure your examples are relevant to the essay question.

sumber daya
Something useful that a country or organization has.
Synonyms: asset, supply, material.
Water is one of the most important natural resources on Earth.

membatasi
To limit or control something.
Synonyms: limit, control, confine.
The new law restricts the sale of cigarettes to people under eighteen.

aman; terjamin
Safe and protected from danger.
Synonyms: safe, protected, stable.
A good education leads to a more secure future.

mencari
To try to find or obtain something.
Synonyms: search, look for, pursue.
Many young people seek opportunities to study abroad.

memilih
To choose carefully from a group.
Synonyms: choose, pick, decide on.
Students must select three subjects to study in their final year.

strategi
A plan to achieve a particular goal.
Synonyms: plan, approach, method.
The teacher explained an effective strategy for answering reading questions.

survei
A study of people's opinions or behavior.
Synonyms: study, poll, investigation.
A recent survey showed that teenagers spend hours on their phones daily.

tradisi
A custom or belief passed down through generations.
Synonyms: custom, practice, heritage.
Indonesia has many beautiful traditions in food and ceremonies.

alternatif
One of two or more choices available.
Synonyms: option, choice, substitute.
Cycling is a healthy alternative to driving short distances.

keadaan; situasi
A condition or fact that affects a situation.
Synonyms: situation, condition, context.
Under normal circumstances, the test is held twice a year.

komponen
One of the parts that something is made of.
Synonyms: part, element, piece.
Vocabulary is a key component of every IELTS section.

persetujuan
Permission for something to happen.
Synonyms: permission, agreement, approval.
Researchers must obtain consent before involving people in studies.

cukup besar; signifikan
Large in amount, size, or importance.
Synonyms: significant, large, substantial.
She has made considerable progress in her speaking skills.

konstan; terus-menerus
Happening all the time or not changing.
Synonyms: continuous, steady, regular.
Constant practice is the secret to mastering a foreign language.

berkontribusi; menyumbang
To help cause or add to something.
Synonyms: add, donate, help.
Burning fossil fuels contributes to global warming.

mengkoordinasikan
To organize different parts to work well together.
Synonyms: organize, arrange, manage.
The teacher coordinates several activities during the school festival.

menunjukkan; mendemonstrasikan
To show clearly that something exists or is true.
Synonyms: show, prove, display.
The experiment demonstrates how plants need sunlight to grow.

dokumen
An official paper or file with information.
Synonyms: paper, record, file.
Please bring all important documents to the visa interview.

penekanan
Special importance given to something.
Synonyms: importance, focus, stress.
Modern schools place greater emphasis on critical thinking skills.

memastikan
To make certain that something happens.
Synonyms: guarantee, make sure, secure.
Please ensure that you arrive at the test center on time.

mengecualikan
To leave something or someone out.
Synonyms: omit, leave out, reject.
The total cost excludes shipping and handling fees.

kerangka kerja
A basic structure or set of ideas to support something.
Synonyms: structure, system, outline.
The new policy provides a clear framework for school inspections.

mengilustrasikan; menggambarkan
To show or explain something with examples.
Synonyms: show, explain, depict.
The chart illustrates the rise in internet use over the last decade.

menerapkan; melaksanakan
To start using a plan or system.
Synonyms: apply, carry out, execute.
The school will implement new online lessons next semester.

menyiratkan; mengimplikasikan
To suggest something without saying it directly.
Synonyms: suggest, hint, indicate.
His silence seemed to imply that he disagreed with the decision.

awal
Happening at the beginning of something.
Synonyms: first, beginning, original.
The initial results of the survey were very promising.

contoh; kejadian
An example or particular case of something.
Synonyms: example, case, occurrence.
This is a clear instance of how technology can save lives.

berinteraksi
To communicate or work together with others.
Synonyms: communicate, engage, connect.
Children learn best when they interact with their classmates.

membenarkan
To give a good reason for something.
Synonyms: defend, support, explain.
You must justify your opinion with strong examples.

memaksimalkan
To make something as large or great as possible.
Synonyms: increase, boost, enlarge.
Good study habits help students maximize their exam scores.

filosofi; filsafat
The study of basic ideas about life and knowledge.
Synonyms: thought, belief, ideology.
Her teaching philosophy focuses on creativity and curiosity.

fisik
Relating to the body or to material things.
Synonyms: bodily, material, tangible.
Physical activity is essential for a healthy lifestyle.

proporsi; bagian
A part of something compared to the whole.
Synonyms: share, percentage, ratio.
A large proportion of students prefer learning through videos.

menerbitkan
To produce a book, article, or website for public reading.
Synonyms: print, release, issue.
The university publishes a new research journal every year.

bereaksi
To behave in a particular way as a response.
Synonyms: respond, reply, answer.
Children often react quickly to changes in their environment.

mendaftar
To enter your name on an official list.
Synonyms: enroll, sign up, record.
You must register for the IELTS test at least one month in advance.

mengandalkan
To depend on someone or something for help.
Synonyms: depend, count on, trust.
Many people rely on public transport to reach their workplace.

skema; rencana
An organized plan, often by the government.
Synonyms: plan, program, project.
The new scheme provides free books to children from poor families.

cukup; memadai
Enough for a particular need.
Synonyms: enough, adequate, ample.
Make sure you get sufficient sleep before the exam day.

tugas
A piece of work to be done.
Synonyms: job, duty, assignment.
Writing Task 2 is the most challenging part of the IELTS exam.

teknik
A particular way of doing something skillfully.
Synonyms: method, skill, approach.
The teacher showed us a useful technique for memorizing new words.

teknologi
The use of science to create useful tools or systems.
Synonyms: science, engineering, innovation.
Technology has transformed the way we learn and communicate.

valid; sah
Acceptable, legal, or based on good reason.
Synonyms: legal, sound, reasonable.
You need a valid passport to travel to another country.

memadai; cukup
Enough or good enough for a purpose.
Synonyms: sufficient, enough, satisfactory.
Many schools lack adequate facilities for science experiments.

sikap
A way of thinking or feeling about something.
Synonyms: outlook, view, mindset.
A positive attitude can help you overcome learning difficulties.

debat; perdebatan
A formal discussion of a topic with different opinions.
Synonyms: discussion, argument, dispute.
There is an ongoing debate about the use of phones in schools.

dalam negeri; rumah tangga
Related to home life or to one's own country.
Synonyms: home, internal, household.
Domestic flights are usually cheaper than international ones.

memungkinkan
To make it possible for someone to do something.
Synonyms: allow, permit, empower.
The internet enables students to access information from anywhere.

generasi
People of about the same age or born around the same time.
Synonyms: age group, era, cohort.
Each generation faces different social and economic challenges.

hipotesis
An idea suggested as an explanation, to be tested.
Synonyms: theory, assumption, proposal.
Scientists test their hypothesis through careful experiments.

secara keseluruhan
Considering everything; in total.
Synonyms: total, general, complete.
The overall quality of education has improved over the last decade.

enggan
Not wanting to do something.
Synonyms: unwilling, hesitant, resistant.
Many students are reluctant to speak in front of the class.

tantangan
Something difficult that tests one's abilities.
Synonyms: difficulty, test, problem.
Living in a foreign country can be a wonderful but tough challenge.

meningkatkan
To improve the quality or value of something.
Synonyms: improve, boost, upgrade.
Reading every day will enhance your vocabulary and writing skills.

menggantikan
To use one thing in place of another.
Synonyms: replace, swap, exchange.
You can substitute butter with olive oil in many healthy recipes.

menopang; mempertahankan
To make something continue for a long time.
Synonyms: maintain, support, continue.
It is hard to sustain motivation throughout a long study program.

kontemporer; modern
Belonging to the present time.
Synonyms: modern, current, present-day.
Contemporary art often reflects the social issues of today.

memanipulasi
To control or change something cleverly, sometimes unfairly.
Synonyms: control, influence, handle.
Some advertisements manipulate viewers' emotions to sell products.

beradaptasi; menyesuaikan
To change in order to fit a new situation.
Synonyms: adjust, modify, fit.
Students must adapt quickly to life at a new university.
